game_info.title: 黄金矿工 - Gold Miner
game_info.concept: **黄金矿工 (Gold Miner)** Players control a stationary miner at the top of the screen, whose claw automatically swings left and right across a 2D mine shaft. A single tap anywhere on the screen deploys the claw downwards, which then retracts, pulling up the first item it hooks. Different items like small gold nuggets ($50), large gold nuggets ($500), rocks ($10), and diamonds ($600) have varying monetary values and weights, with heavier items significantly slowing the claw's retraction speed by up to 50%. Each level presents a specific money target, such as $500, which players must achieve within a strict time limit, typically 60 seconds, displayed on the UI. Successfully meeting the target advances the player to the next level, where money targets increase by approximately $100 and item layouts become more complex, while failure results in a game over. After each level, players can access a shop to spend earned money on single-use power-ups like dynamite to destroy unwanted grabbed items or strength potions to temporarily increase claw retraction speed by 50%.
game_info.description: You are a gold miner trying to reach money targets within time limits! Control a swinging claw that automatically moves left and right at the top of the mine. Deploy the claw to grab valuable items from below - gold nuggets, diamonds, and rocks. Each item has different values and weights affecting how fast the claw retracts. Meet the level target before time runs out to advance. Between levels, visit the shop to buy power-ups like dynamite to destroy unwanted items or strength potions to speed up retraction. Progress through increasingly challenging levels with higher targets!
game_info.controls:
SPACE: Deploy/release the claw to grab items
D: Use dynamite (destroys currently grabbed item, purchased from shop)
S: Use strength potion (speeds up claw retraction, purchased from shop)
ENTER: Start game / Continue to next level / Enter shop
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic claw deployment and item collection mechanics. Verifies that the claw swings properly, deploys when triggered, grabs items on contact, and retracts with the item. Tests that money is added correctly based on item values and that heavier items slow down retraction speed as intended.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Deploy the claw when it's centered over various items including small gold, large gold, rocks, and diamonds. Observe the retraction speed differences between light and heavy items. Verify that the money counter increases by the correct amount when items are successfully retrieved. Test multiple grab cycles to ensure the system resets properly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The claw should swing smoothly, deploy on command, grab the first item it contacts, and retract at speeds varying by item weight. Money should increase correctly for each item type. The test is successful when all item types can be grabbed, money is awarded accurately, and the claw resets for the next deployment without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing level progression and win condition. Verifies that reaching the money target within the time limit triggers level completion, displays the shop screen, and allows progression to the next level with increased difficulty. Tests that the target increases appropriately and items respawn for the new level.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Rapidly deploy the claw to grab high-value items like diamonds and large gold nuggets. Focus on reaching the money target (starting at $500) before the 60-second timer expires. When the level completes, verify the shop appears. Skip shop purchases and proceed to the next level. Observe that the new level has a higher target and new item layout.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Upon reaching the money target, the game should transition to a shop screen showing available power-ups. Continuing should start the next level with an increased target (approximately +$100). The test is successful when multiple levels can be completed in sequence, targets increase correctly, and items respawn in new positions each level.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing time limit and lose condition. Verifies that failing to reach the money target within the 60-second time limit triggers a game over state. Tests that the game properly displays the lose screen with final statistics and allows restart via the R key.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally grab only low-value items like rocks or deliberately miss items to waste time. Let the timer run down to zero without reaching the target. Observe the game over screen appearance and verify the displayed statistics. Press R to return to the start screen and confirm proper game reset.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When time reaches zero without meeting the target, the game should display a game over screen showing final money earned and the target that was missed. The R key should reset the game to the start screen. The test is successful when the lose condition triggers correctly and the game can be restarted cleanly.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing shop and power-up purchase system. Verifies that players can purchase power-ups between levels, that money is deducted correctly, that owned items are displayed, and that power-ups cannot be purchased without sufficient funds.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Complete a level to access the shop. Attempt to purchase dynamite and strength potions, verifying that money is deducted. Try to purchase items when funds are insufficient. Proceed to the next level and verify that purchased power-ups are shown in the UI with their quantities. Exit and re-enter the shop to ensure persistence within the game session.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The shop should display available power-ups with prices. Purchases should deduct money and increase item counts. Insufficient funds should prevent purchases. Owned power-ups should appear in the game UI. The test is successful when all purchase mechanics work correctly and item counts persist between shop visits.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing dynamite power-up functionality. Verifies that using dynamite (D key) destroys the currently grabbed item, allows the claw to retract empty at normal speed, and properly decrements the dynamite count. Tests that dynamite cannot be used when none are owned or when no item is grabbed.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Purchase dynamite from the shop. During gameplay, deploy the claw to grab a heavy item like large gold. While the claw is retracting with the item, press D to use dynamite. Observe that the item disappears and the claw speeds up. Try using dynamite when the claw is empty or swinging. Verify the dynamite count decreases only when successfully used.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Pressing D while holding an item should destroy it, show a visual effect, reset the claw to retract empty, and decrement the dynamite count. Using dynamite when invalid should have no effect. The test is successful when dynamite works as intended and cannot be exploited or used in invalid states.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing strength potion power-up functionality. Verifies that using a strength potion (S key) temporarily increases claw retraction speed for a limited duration, properly decrements the potion count, and displays the remaining duration. Tests that the effect expires correctly and returns speed to normal.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Purchase strength potions from the shop. During gameplay, press S to activate a potion. Deploy the claw to grab heavy items and observe the increased retraction speed. Monitor the duration indicator to confirm the effect is active. Wait for the effect to expire and grab another heavy item to verify normal speed has returned. Test that potions cannot be used when none are owned.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Activating a strength potion should increase retraction speed by 50% for a fixed duration (e.g., 10 seconds), decrement the potion count, and display a timer. After expiration, speed should return to normal. The test is successful when the potion provides the correct speed boost, duration is accurate, and the effect doesn't stack or persist incorrectly.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game phase transitions. Verifies that pressing ESC during gameplay pauses the game, freezes all movement and timers, displays a pause overlay, and resumes correctly when ESC is pressed again. Tests that other controls are disabled during pause.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Start a game and deploy the claw. While the claw is moving, press ESC to pause. Verify that the claw position freezes and the timer stops. Attempt to press SPACE, D, and S to confirm controls are disabled. Press ESC again to unpause and verify the game resumes from the exact state. Test pausing at different game moments including claw swinging, deploying, and retracting.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Pressing ESC should immediately freeze all game elements, stop the timer, and display a "PAUSED" overlay. No gameplay controls should function during pause. Pressing ESC again should resume smoothly. The test is successful when pause/unpause works reliably at any game moment without causing desynchronization or bugs.
